---
title: '2014/01/18 Journal Sat'
date: '2014-01-18T15:52:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '72891479866733279'
blogger_filename: '/2014/01/20140118-journal-sat.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Journal'
---

<h4>
Feeling</h4>
Hungry. Does that count as a feeling? Blank on feelings for the moment.<br />
<h4>
Thankful</h4>
Thankful for last night. Very cool time with my wife. Thankful for her insight into me, and my obsession with Wells Fargo. Thankful for the things that are yet to come this year...<br />
<h4>
What's Happening?</h4>
Had a good time with my wife last night. Which didn't look like it was going to happen, but then it did. That was cool. We went to the car ready to "go out" for our anniversary while my mom watched the kids. Go out and do what? Maybe she should have taken them out while we stayed home? That was our thought. What do people without kids do? We had no clue how to spend several hours without kids. She was falling asleep in the passenger seat. LOL.<br />
<br />
We ended up at Starbucks. We got Sopapillas from Rosa's Cafe and then sat at Starbucks. She happened to have cards with her and we played Rummy. Then we talked. About kids, life, goals, stuff. It was good because we don't talk like that often... at all. So maybe I'll have to make a point of pulling out the cards and playing a game one night a week... or more. Then we went and saw the Hobbit II. That was fun!<br />
<br />
Came to the conclusion that my obsession with Wells Fargo is unhealthy. It's just like how with my Mom you could say... "Oh the sky is blue" and she'd say "Oh it IS blue... except at Billies..." or with Uncle Dave "Oh it is blue... and let me tell you more about me and my career that I'm pursuing to the death of my family."<br />
<br />
We all, as humans, love to talk about ourselves. We tend to galvanize around certain things that are our biggest problems. We need to take time to re-frame those experiences and focus back to God. I need to allow God's Grace to extend to Wells Fargo. How is God going to bring change when I'm cursing them every day?<br />
<br />
From now on, when I think of WF and the wrong they do to employees and customers what WILL I say?<br />
<br />
<h4>
VOD:</h4>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<br />
<a href="https://www.bible.com/users/darrellwolfe/reading-plans/28-joseph-prince?initial=true&amp;user_id=darrellwolfe" target="_blank">Romans 5:17 </a><span class="quiet" style="color: #444444; font-family: &quot;Helvetica Neue&quot;, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 21px;"><a href="https://www.bible.com/users/darrellwolfe/reading-plans/28-joseph-prince?initial=true&amp;user_id=darrellwolfe" target="_blank">LEB &nbsp;</a></span><strong>17</strong> For if by the trespass of the one man, death reigned through 
the one man, much more will those who receive the abundance of grace and of the 
gift of righteousness reign in life through the one, Jesus Christ. &nbsp;</blockquote>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
Thought For The Day from Joseph Prince<br />
The more we believe in His grace, the more we will reign 
over all forms of death!&nbsp;</blockquote>
<br />
<br />
To Wells Fargo I will say? Death to you evil company! J/K. That's what I've been saying. Literally. That was the first thought that came to mind. Reframe. I will say?<br />
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<b>Grace to you WF, and may the all you need to prosper with GOOD success come to light!&nbsp;</b></blockquote>
DW<br />
<br />
<br />
KCM... this one is worth keeping!<br />
<br />
<table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width: 100%px;"><tbody>
<tr>
<td style="padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-top: 10px;">
<div style="background-color: #e5eaee; background-image: url(http://webr.emv2.com/kcm/email/f2f/en/bg.jpg); background-repeat: repeat; padding-bottom: 10px; padding-left: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-top: 10px;">
<div style="background-color: white; border: 1px solid rgb(214, 227, 233); color: #666666; font-size: 16px; padding: 10px 20px;">
Can 
two walk together, except they be agreed? - Amos 3:3</div>
</div>
</td></tr>
<tr>
<td style="padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 15px; padding-right: 15px; padding-top: 0px;">
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
When 
it comes to faith, a lot of believers feel like they have one foot nailed to the 
ground. No matter how hard they try, they just can't seem to make any 
progress.</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
If 
you'll watch them, you can see why. They literally haven't "gotten themselves 
together." One minute they'll be telling you, "Oh yes, amen, I believe the 
Word," and the next minute they'll be spouting unbelief like it's going out of 
style. "I know God says He'll prosper us, but I'll tell you what, my business is 
doing so badly, it's about to give me ulcers. I can't sleep at night for 
worrying."</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
Dig 
a little deeper and you may find out that they've pulled their actions out of 
line with the Word as well. "Well, you understand, brother, I can't possibly 
tithe with my income like it is. I'd go under!"</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
Faith 
just won't work for a person like that.</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
You 
see, you're a triune being. You are a spirit. You have a soul, which consists of 
your mind, will, and emotions. And you live in a body. Each of those areas has a 
specific role to play in your faith walk. You have to get all three in agreement 
before you can go anywhere at all!</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<b>Start </b>
by feeding your spirit on the Word of God. Just like the body produces physical 
strength when you nourish it with food, the spirit produces spiritual strength 
when you nourish it with the Word. That spiritual strength is called faith. 
Develop that faith, and instead of your spirit being dominated by the other two 
areas, it will be the one in charge.</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<b>Next</b>, 
bring your soul in line. Set your mind on "things above." Meditate on the Word 
until your thoughts begin to agree with it. Keep your attention on it until even 
your emotions yield.</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<b>Finally</b>, 
bring your body in line. Once you truly get your spirit and soul established on 
the Word, that won't be hard. The body is a follower, not a leader. It will do 
whatever you train it to do. Begin teaching your body to act on the truth you've 
planted in your mind and spirit, and it will follow right along.</div>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
Don't 
try to walk in faith with your foot nailed to the ground. Get yourself together! 
Bring your spirit, soul, and body in harmony--and the Word will take you as far 
as you want to go.</div>
<em>By Kenneth Copeland</em></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<br />
<h4>
<b>Start&nbsp;</b></h4>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
by feeding your spirit on the Word of God. Just like the body produces physical strength when you nourish it with food, the spirit produces spiritual strength when you nourish it with the Word. That spiritual strength is called faith. Develop that faith, and instead of your spirit being dominated by the other two areas, it will be the one in charge.</div>
<h4>
<b>Next</b>,&nbsp;</h4>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
bring your soul in line. Set your mind on "things above." Meditate on the Word until your thoughts begin to agree with it. Keep your attention on it until even your emotions yield.</div>
<h4>
<b>Finally</b>,</h4>
<div style="margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
&nbsp;bring your body in line. Once you truly get your spirit and soul established on the Word, that won't be hard. The body is a follower, not a leader. It will do whatever you train it to do. Begin teaching your body to act on the truth you've planted in your mind and spirit, and it will follow right along.</div>
<br />
